title: Directly Compare Object Lengths
description: Compare the lengths of multiple objects and determine their relative order from shortest to longest.
condition: Given two or more objects with visibly different lengths.
response: Determine which is shorter or longer and order them accordingly.
label: Production / Skill
example: If rectangle B is shorter than A and C, and A is shorter than C, then order is B, A, C.
notes: Orientation may differ; comparison is based on length, not position.

title: Measure Length by Counting Units
description: Measure an object's length by counting the number of equal-length units that span from one endpoint to the other.
condition: An object is aligned with connecting cubes from its start point to its end point.
response: Count only the cubes that align with the object's length.
label: Production / Skill
example: If 7 cubes span the rectangle from start to end, the length is 7 units.

title: Represent Start Unknown with Subtraction Equation
description: Write an equation of the form ? − a = b to represent a Take From, Start Unknown story.
condition: Story describes an unknown starting amount, a known amount taken away, and a known result.
response: Write equation ? − a = b using a symbol for the unknown.
label: Rule / Plan
example: There were ? students. 7 left and 6 remain: $? - 7 = 6$.
notes: Mapping story structure to equation form.

title: Use Transitive Comparison
description: Use known comparisons between objects to infer a new comparison.
condition: Given statements such as A is shorter than B and B is shorter/longer than C.
response: Infer and state the relationship between A and C.
label: Principle / Model
example: If the noodle is shorter than the pencil and the straw is longer than the pencil, then the straw is longer than the noodle.
